description_long: This is a rectangular, three-story brick apartment building with a flat, slightly recessed roof and a symmetrical facade. The exterior is constructed from reddish-brown bricks, with decorative brickwork above and below the windows. Each floor features a row of large, multi-pane windows with white or faded blue frames, some of which appear weathered or partially obscured. A prominent black drainpipe runs vertically along the facade, adding to the utilitarian aesthetic. The building lacks balconies and has no visible ornamentation beyond the brickwork and window framing. The overall appearance suggests an early 20th-century industrial or utilitarian architectural style, commonly found in urban neighborhoods.
description: A three-story, red-brick apartment building with large, multi-pane windows and a flat roof, featuring a symmetrical, utilitarian facade.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main facade with six large windows per floor, a central black drainpipe, and detailed brickwork.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing the same window arrangement and drainpipe, with subtle perspective on the brick texture.
description_view_2: Further rotated view, maintaining the symmetrical window layout and central drainpipe, with more shadow on the right side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the same window and brick arrangement but with less visible drainpipe detail.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a symmetrical grid pattern, with three vertically aligned windows per floor on each side of the central drainpipe, evenly spaced and grouped in rows.
